message: dep-guard.sh captures `go list` stderr into the dependency list (`2>&1`) and, on the success path, iterates that same variable as the list of dependencies. Any line go list writes to stderr while exiting 0 (e.g. `go: downloading <module>` on a cold module cache) is treated as a non-stdlib dependency, matches no allowed prefix, and triggers a spurious non-zero exit even though no forbidden import exists.
trigger: Running the generalized script against a pattern whose dependencies require module download (or any other condition that makes `go list` write to stderr while exiting 0), e.g. a cold module cache emitting `go: downloading ...`.
fix_direction: Capture stderr separately from stdout (e.g. redirect stderr to a temp file or a second variable); build the dependency list from stdout only, and surface the captured stderr solely when `go list` exits non-zero.
uncertainty: The trigger does not fire for this repo's current stdlib+internal-only dependency graph, so the spurious failure is latent; it would manifest only when the generalized script is pointed at packages with downloadable external module deps or other stderr-emitting conditions.
evidence: Line 14: `deps=$(go list -deps -test ... "$pattern" 2>&1)` merges stderr into $deps. When rc==0 the failure branch (lines 16-19) is skipped and $deps is consumed verbatim as the dependency list by the while-loop (lines 22-31); a non-prefix-matching line is appended to `offending`, causing `exit 1` (lines 33-37). This repo has only stdlib + internal deps (verified via go.mod and `go list`), so go list is silent today, but the script is now generalized for arbitrary patterns/prefixes.

message: No prompt test asserts the moderator rules-of-engagement text. The acceptance criteria require the rendered prompt to contain the moderator rules, the brief, the board, and the signal instruction; tests cover brief, board, and signal instruction, but the moderator rules (prompt.go:12 moderatorRules, written at prompt.go:35) are never asserted. Deleting moderatorRules would leave all prompt tests green.
trigger: always — a regression removing or altering the moderator rules-of-engagement output ships undetected because no test exercises it.
fix_direction: Add an assertion that the rendered prompt contains a distinctive moderator-rules substring (e.g. "structured deliberation" or "State your current position").
evidence: grep for moderator|deliberation|structured|blocking objection|state your over prompt_test.go returns no matches. The closest assertion, prompt_test.go:39, checks for the substring "done", which is also present in signalInstruction, so it does not cover moderatorRules.

message: Quorum's strictly-more-than-half boundary is untested. The only quorum test (TestVerdictSettledQuorum) uses 2-of-3 done, which is clearly above half. No test covers the exact-half case (e.g. 1-of-2 or 2-of-4 done in Quorum mode) failing to be Clean, so the boundary at verdict.go:52 (doneCount*2 > n) is unverified and a > to >= mutation would survive the suite.
trigger: always — the strict-majority boundary that defines quorum is not pinned by a test, so an off-by-one in the comparison would not be caught.
fix_direction: Add a quorum test where exactly half of speakers are done (e.g. 1-of-2 or 2-of-4) and assert the round is not Clean / does not settle.
evidence: verdict.Quorum appears only at verdict_test.go:81 (TestVerdictSettledQuorum, 2 of 3 done). verdict.go:52 implements clean = n > 0 && doneCount*2 > n; the exact-half non-clean case is never asserted.

message: The open-objection set semantics (union across speakers; order and duplicates immaterial) are not exercised. Every Progress-related test (TestVerdictStalemate, TestVerdictMax, TestVerdictProgressTracking) scripts the same single objection for both speakers, so the union of distinct objections across speakers and the order/duplicate-independence of the set comparison are never tested. A bug comparing objection lists positionally or per-speaker rather than as a merged string set could survive.
trigger: Multi-speaker rounds where speakers raise different objections, or the same set reordered across rounds — paths the suite never constructs.
fix_direction: Add a verdict test where two speakers raise different objections in one round (objSet is their union) and a follow-up round whose union is the same set in a different per-speaker arrangement, asserting Progress=false.
uncertainty: The implementation appears correct via map-based set semantics; this is a coverage gap rather than a confirmed runtime defect.
evidence: verdict.go:43-46 builds objSet as a union over all parsed signals' Objections; verdict.go:57/76 compare it as a set. All test scripts (e.g. verdict_test.go:197-198) give both speakers an identical single objection, so objSet is always a single element and the cross-speaker union plus order/duplicate handling are untested.
